reviewer_prior_involvement_notes: OpenAI provider authored the intake and stage-design artifacts and acted as the earlier bookkeeper. Actual dispatched model: gpt-5.6-sol with xhigh reasoning effort. The claimed override could not be verified because the Gemini evidence contains an operator statement rather than the required runner-level availability-check failure artifact.

findings.title: Strong-reviewer override lacks the required runner-level availability evidence
findings.evidence: The artifact contains an operator statement that Gemini is frequently unreachable, but no concrete runner-level attempt, exit result, raw output, or receipt. AGENTS.md:223-229 requires a failed runner-level check and recorded evidence.
findings.impact: The design-conflicted OpenAI reviewer cannot produce an accepting Review-2 gate under the documented override.
findings.recommendation: Run and preserve the configured runner-level availability check for an unrelated reviewer, or route Review-2 to an eligible unrelated provider.

findings.title: Authorization scope, commit state, forbidden paths, and budgets are not bound to execution
findings.evidence: Preflight checks field presence but does not require committed authorization/approval evidence or exact matching of task_ids, topology, pathspecs, and budgets. A temporary-repository counterexample passed with authorization limited to T1 and one call while status contained T2 and 99 calls, the authorization was dirty, and a forbidden path was modified.
findings.impact: Mutable status or an uncommitted artifact can broaden tasks, model calls, and write scope beyond human authorization.
findings.recommendation: Require committed authorization and approval evidence, bind all exact-match fields, enforce forbidden pathspecs, and use authorization values as runtime authority.

findings.title: Production registry command templates are incompatible with runner substitution
findings.evidence: The runner substitutes @PROMPT@/@REPO@ while agents/registry.yaml uses <prompt-file>/<repo>; the line parser also leaves YAML escape characters. Tests inject synthetic @PROMPT@ templates instead of the production registry.
findings.impact: Real Claude-GLM, Kimi, and Grok invocations receive malformed or literal placeholders, so the automatic pipeline is not operational.
findings.recommendation: Use one frozen placeholder convention, parse command values correctly, avoid unsafe shell expansion, and test real registry command construction without live calls.

findings.title: Verdict validation is weaker than the schema and accepted bytes are rewritten
findings.evidence: The runtime validator accepts unknown top-level fields and invalid required_fixes item types rejected by the frozen schema. _store_verdict reserializes the object instead of preserving the accepted source bytes.
findings.impact: Schema-invalid model output can drive transitions, and the stored verdict is not the unaltered P3 evidence.
findings.recommendation: Implement schema-equivalent validation and persist the exact accepted JSON byte span, with adversarial parity and byte-equality tests.

findings.title: Automatic fixes do not charge the authoritative stage ledger and expiry is not rechecked
findings.evidence: _charge_auto_change updates only nested auto_code_changes_used and never top-level rework_count. expires_at is checked only in initial preflight, not before every model call and commit.
findings.impact: Automatic and Review-2 repairs do not share the frozen max_rework=3 ledger, and expired authorization can continue to drive calls or commits.
findings.recommendation: Atomically charge both counters and enforce authorization expiry immediately before every invocation and git commit.

findings.title: Exclusive runner locking and the real H_snapshot crash window are unimplemented
findings.evidence: No registered-worktree or active-runner lock check exists. H_snapshot is committed before snapshot_commit is persisted, while recovery depends on that marker; the test manually writes the marker and skips the actual crash window.
findings.impact: Concurrent runners can race, and a crash immediately after H_snapshot cannot deterministically recover the exact review snapshot.
findings.recommendation: Add runtime lock ownership/recovery and journal or safely infer the exact snapshot across every injected crash boundary.

findings.title: Runner restart and adapter-error paths can repeat or advance invalid work
findings.evidence: States other than awaiting_human are treated as a new authorization and every unit restarts at implementation. Nonzero or timed-out implementation results are recorded but do not stop the pipeline before blocking and seal.
findings.impact: Restarting can redispatch accepted units, while a failed implementation may reach seal if existing checks pass.
findings.recommendation: Persist idempotent node progress, reject invalid start states, skip completed units, and route adapter errors through frozen fallback/escalation transitions.

findings.title: Authoritative status retains obsolete routing and blocker records
findings.evidence: model_routing still says T1 correction is ready and Review-1 is blocked, and lines 732-735 list closed T1-R2 findings despite status review_2 and three task ACCEPT records.
findings.impact: Machine consumers receive contradictory blocker and next-hop state, while the current validator passes it.
findings.recommendation: Reconcile routing/blocker fields and add validator invariants for stage-state consistency.

required_fixes: Provide valid runner-level unrelated-reviewer unavailability evidence or select a genuinely unrelated final-review provider.
Enforce committed human authorization and exact scope, topology, adapter, pathspec, forbidden-path, and budget binding in runner and validator.
Make production registry command parsing and placeholder substitution correct and test the real registry path.
Make runtime verdict validation schema-equivalent and store the accepted verdict byte span unaltered.
Charge every automatic code-changing fix to top-level rework_count and recheck authorization expiry before every model call and commit.
Implement an exclusive runner lock and close the H_snapshot-before-status crash-recovery gap.
Make runner resume/idempotency and adapter-error transitions fail closed.
Remove obsolete blockers/routing values and add status-consistency validator tests.
Rerun the complete deterministic suite, reseal affected units, recompute fingerprints, and repeat the required review gates.
residual_risks: A1 Authority Order remains a deferred follow-up and is acceptable only after the required fixes close.
The approximate pathspec matcher under-matches exotic patterns and currently fails closed; retain explicit documentation and tests.
The registry model-version metadata remains a non-blocking follow-up after the control-plane defects are fixed.
